rulings.Case Title: CCSF VS VAN UPP
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 8, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT ARDEN VAN UPP'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Hearing Required. The record reflects that the Judge hearing this motion was the Chief Trial Deputy when the default was taken. The Judge had no involvement in this case and does not believe that he must recuse himself from hearing this matter. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. CHARLES M. COLLINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Amend Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 8, 2008, line 3. PLAINTIFF PAUL VANDER WAERDT'S Motion To Amend Complaint is denied without prejudice. Given the Order granting Summary Adjudication, plaintiff must clarify what the proposed amended complaint will allege. Plaintiff must address the reason for the delay in seeking the amendment. Plaintiff must seek relief from the Bankruptcy Court to prosecute the misrepresentation/insurance claim or demonstrate why the claim is based only on "any available insurance coverage." (Order of Feb. 21, 2006 U.S. Bankruptcy Court). =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH KOSICH et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 8, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT NAVISTAR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication are denied. Dr. Berman's testimony confirms that he did not diagnose plaintiff as having an asbestos disease but sent him to a specialist. (Depo 29-30). Construing the evidence in favor of plaintiff, he did not have reason to know of the asbestos caused disease until Dr. Masters' diagnosis. Navistar's sophisticated user argument is without merit. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: BROADWAY PROPERTIES LLC VS. WE PARK, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Completion Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 8, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANT WE PARK, INC'S Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Completion Of Arbitration. The motion to stay is granted. Based on the Court's ruling in case # CUD-08-625613, Powell Partners is an indespensable party in this case. Given the dispute amonG the Powell Partners and the arbitration provisions of that partnership agreement, it is essential that the parties resolve those issues before this litigation proceeds. The record does not reflect that Broadway Properties will be adversely affected by the delay. =(302/PJM/XJL)
